steps.title: Identify the key signal about Camila's understanding
steps.content: The critical detail is in the final sentence: “She knows from her teaching that this is not a good sign, the use of pencil intended to soften the indignity of the corrections.” Camila understands that the penciled approach is a deliberate softening of criticism—gentle phrasing meant to cushion bad news.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice C states her exact understanding: she is “aware that it is meant to be a sensitive way of indicating the faults.” The others misread the situation: A treats sparse praise as merit-spotting, B invents gratitude and calls indirect feedback direct, D fabricates disagreement. That matches choice C — C is correct.
